description: Most relevant base data around poverty and inequality, drawn from published science: absolute, relative, extreme, subjective and multidimensional poverty; the poverty line and threshold including the World Bank international poverty line of 2.15 USD per person per day (2017 PPP); the Gini coefficient and income inequality; deprivation, wealth distribution, economic mobility and the poverty trap. The formulas reproduce three relationships in the calc-validation block: the extreme-poverty headcount rate as the poor share of the world population (World Bank 2024), the relative poverty line as 60 percent of the median income (Eurostat), and the income quintile share ratio (S80/S20). Base words owned by other files (income, consumption, Gini, share, ratio, rate, mobility, world, population, person, percent, USD) are re-declared name-only so the import does not overwrite their canonical description; the poverty-specific words carry a Wikipedia ref plus the cleaned first sentence of the lead as the description.

words.name: poverty
words.description: the state of having insufficient income or resources to meet basic human needs such as food, shelter and clothing.

words.name: PPP
words.description: purchasing power parity: a way of comparing currencies by the amount of goods and services each can buy, used to make cross-country income comparisons.

words.name: inequality
words.description: the unequal distribution of income, wealth or opportunity among individuals or groups within a society.

words.name: wealth
words.description: the total value of the assets owned by a person, community or country, minus liabilities.

words.name: MPI
words.description: the multidimensional poverty index, a measure that captures the deprivations people face in health, education and living standards.

words.name: European Union
words.description: a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.

words.name: deprivation
words.description: the lack of resources or conditions regarded as necessary for an adequate standard of living

words.name: absolute
words.description: measured against a fixed standard, not relative to others

words.name: relative
words.description: measured in relation to others in the same society

words.name: extreme
words.description: the most severe degree

words.name: subjective
words.description: based on personal perception or self-assessment

words.name: international
words.description: applying across countries

words.name: multidimensional
words.description: covering several dimensions such as health, education and living standards

words.name: median
words.description: the middle value that separates the higher half from the lower half

words.name: line
words.description: a fixed level used as a boundary

words.name: threshold
words.description: a level at which something begins or changes

words.name: trap
words.description: a situation that is difficult to escape

words.name: distribution
words.description: the way something is shared across a population

words.name: economic
words.description: relating to the economy, income and wealth

words.name: top
words.description: the highest part or group

words.name: bottom
words.description: the lowest part or group

triples.name: absolute poverty
triples.description: poverty measured against a fixed minimum standard of basic needs

triples.name: relative poverty
triples.description: poverty measured relative to the typical income of the surrounding society

triples.name: extreme poverty
triples.description: living below the international poverty line of 2.15 USD per person per day

triples.name: subjective poverty
triples.description: poverty based on people's own assessment of their situation

triples.name: multidimensional poverty
triples.description: poverty measured across health, education and living-standard deprivations

triples.name: poverty line
triples.description: the income or consumption level below which a person is counted as poor

triples.name: poverty threshold
triples.description: the level of income or consumption that marks the boundary of poverty

triples.name: international poverty line
triples.description: the World Bank line for extreme poverty, 2.15 USD per person per day in 2017 PPP

triples.name: relative poverty line
triples.description: a poverty line set at a fraction, often 60 percent, of the median income

triples.name: economic mobility
triples.description: the ability of a person or family to improve their economic status over time

triples.name: poverty trap
triples.description: a self-reinforcing mechanism that keeps people in poverty

triples.name: poverty rate
triples.description: the share of a population living below the poverty line

triples.name: top income share
triples.description: the share of total income received by the richest 20 percent

triples.name: bottom income share
triples.description: the share of total income received by the poorest 20 percent

triples.name: income ratio
triples.description: the income quintile share ratio S80/S20, the top income share divided by the bottom income share

sources.name: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform 2024
sources.description: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform: about 692 million people (around 8.5 percent) lived in extreme poverty below 2.15 USD per day (2017 PPP) in 2024; global Gini coefficient about 0.62.

sources.name: UNDP OPHI Global MPI 2024
sources.description: UNDP and Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative, Global Multidimensional Poverty Index 2024: about 1.1 billion people are multidimensionally poor across more than 110 developing countries.

sources.name: Eurostat Income and Living Conditions 2023
sources.description: Eurostat, EU Statistics on Income and Living Conditions: the at-risk-of-poverty threshold is set at 60 percent of the national median equivalised income; the income quintile share ratio (S80/S20) is about 5.

values.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform 2024
